DESCRIPTION:
Measures the Round-Trip Time (RTT) from the local machine to the server.
        Ensures that latency is within acceptable limits for a stable VPN connection.
NAME: Network: Latency and Packet Loss (Client to Server)

DESCRIPTION:
REQ-004: OS Standardization (Ubuntu/Debian).
        Logic: Verify that the server runs a supported Linux distribution.
        This prevents 'configuration drift' where servers become too different to manage.
NAME: test_os_version[NL-AMS]

DESCRIPTION:
REQ-001: SSH Root Login must be disabled.
        Logic: Check /etc/ssh/sshd_config for 'PermitRootLogin no'.
        (Marked as xfail: Currently we use root user for automation, architectural exception).
NAME: test_ssh_root_login_disabled[RU-MOW]

DESCRIPTION: Standardization check: Ensures the node runs a supported Linux version.
NAME: Audit: OS Distribution Compliance

DESCRIPTION:
Verifies Fail2Ban service.
        If it is missing or stopped, the test attempts to fix the system automatically.
NAME: Audit: Fail2Ban Service with Self-Healing

DESCRIPTION: Ensures that the Uncomplicated Firewall (UFW) is active and protecting the node.
NAME: Audit: Firewall (UFW) Status

DESCRIPTION:

DESCRIPTION:
REQ-001: SSH Root Login must be disabled.

DESCRIPTION:
Verifies the server's internet bandwidth by downloading a test file from a Global CDN.
        This simulates real-world heavy usage (e.g., 4K video streaming).
NAME: Network: Download Speed via Global CDN

DESCRIPTION:
Verifies Fail2Ban service.
        If it is missing or stopped, the test attempts to fix the system automatically.
NAME: Audit: Fail2Ban Service with Self-Healing

DESCRIPTION:
REQ-003: Intrusion Prevention System (Fail2Ban).
        Self-Healing 2.0: Install if missing, AND restart if stopped/crashed.
NAME: test_fail2ban_status[NL-AMS]

DESCRIPTION: Ensures that the Uncomplicated Firewall (UFW) is active and protecting the node.
NAME: Audit: Firewall (UFW) Status

DESCRIPTION:
REQ-002: Firewall (UFW) must be active.
        Logic: We check the command output 'ufw status' explicitly.
        We expect to see 'Status: active'. This covers both Ubuntu and Debian correctly,
        avoiding false negatives from systemd service status.
